draft.introduction: Jesus reads Isaiah 61 in the Nazareth synagogue and declares “Today this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.” His hometown audience turns violent—the first rejection that foreshadows the cross.

translation.text: And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. And as was his custom, he went to the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and he stood up to read. And the scroll of the prophet Isaiah was given to him. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written, “The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor.” And he rolled up the scroll and said, “Today this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.”
translation.original_text: σήμερον πεπλήρωται ἡ γραφὴ αὕτη ἐν τοῖς ὠσὶν ὑμῶν

translator_notes.note: “Today” (σήμερον) is a key Lukan word, appearing repeatedly at decisive moments (2:11, 19:9, 23:43). Luke’s theology emphasizes the present reality of salvation—not merely a future hope but a “today” event.
